reflection.episodic_summary: Agent attempted to retrieve heart rate data for patient S6212774 but used an incorrect date format in the API call, resulting in an invalid action and episode failure.
reflection.failure_analysis: The agent failed because it constructed a FHIR API query with a malformed date parameter ('ge2023-11-07T10:47:00Z' missing a space or proper encoding), causing the request to be rejected as invalid.
action_guidelines: [P1] When constructing FHIR date parameters, ensure proper formatting: use 'ge' prefix followed by a space or proper ISO 8601 date string (e.g., 'ge2023-11-07T10:47:00Z' should be 'ge 2023-11-07T10:47:00Z').
[P1] Before executing an API call, validate the full URL for syntax errors, especially date/time parameters and URL encoding.
[P1] If a query returns no data, double-check the parameter syntax and the actual available data range before concluding absence of data.

kept: [P1] When verifying active opioid orders, always search by therapeutic class (e.g., 'opioid analgesics') in addition to individual drug names to capture all formulations and coding variants.
[P1] Compare all retrieved values against protocol-defined thresholds before deciding on action.
[P1] Treat absence only after exhaustive search; do not assume from a single resource type or code system.

[P1] When verifying active medications, search across all relevant medication types, resource types (MedicationRequest, MedicationAdministration, MedicationDispense), and code systems (e.g., RxNorm, generic, brand, drug class) and confirm search completeness before concluding absence.
[P1] When searching for procedures, use standard coding systems (e.g., SNOMED CT, LOINC) not local codes unless confirmed valid.
[P1] When creating a FHIR resource, validate against the server's required fields by reviewing prior successful POST examples or error responses.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.
[P1] Do not assume a limited set of codes covers all possible representations of a procedure; use broad text or concept searches as fallback.

[P1] Check for existing orders (e.g., removal orders) before creating a conflicting or duplicate request, searching across all relevant resource types (ServiceRequest, Procedure, Observation) and statuses (active, completed, entered-in-error) using appropriate removal codes or generic terms.
[P1] If a requested lab has no results in records, explicitly note its absence and do not attempt protocol action that depends on those values.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch all available results for each requested test, then select the most recent by date.

[P1] Before assessing whether a time threshold is met, retrieve the exact start timestamp from the relevant resource (e.g., Procedure, ServiceRequest) before comparing with current time; do not assume from absence or incomplete search.
[P1] Validate API endpoint and parameters before making a call; avoid invalid actions that abort the episode.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, explicitly list all matching dates from search results and pick the maximum.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields, invalid values, resource type mismatches, or coding system issues.
[P1] When verifying medication pairings, retrieve all active orders for the primary drug class first, then cross-check each against the secondary drug class.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.

[P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps for the maximum required range first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately; if no data exists for a window, return an explicit indicator (e.g., 'No data') rather than a fabricated value.
[P1] When searching for a procedure by name, verify the returned procedure code matches the exact requested exam type, not a related or reference-only variant.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) not just text.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent searched for COVID-19 vaccination records, found none, and incorrectly assumed last dose was >12 months ago, then ordered a booster. Correct action would be to report no records found and not proceed with ordering.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly inferred a missing record implies a past dose >12 months ago. Without any record, there is no basis to determine the last dose date or that it exceeds the threshold. The agent should report absence of data and refrain from ordering.
action_guidelines: [P1] If no records exist for a required data point, report 'No records found' and do not proceed with actions that depend on that data.
[P1] Do not infer a date or time threshold from the absence of data; absence does not imply any specific value.
[P1] Before ordering based on a time threshold, confirm that at least one record exists with a date to compare against the threshold.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent searched for catheter procedure within 48 hours, found none, and concluded no removal needed. However, the task required checking for a catheter in place >48 hours without a removal order—a different temporal logic. The agent misapplied the time window.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly used a 48-hour window for the procedure placement instead of checking if the catheter was in place for more than 48 hours and lacked a documented removal order. The search logic was reversed.
action_guidelines: [P1] When checking duration of an active device or procedure, search for all instances regardless of start time, then filter by duration since placement, not by a recent time window.
[P1] For removal-required conditions, verify absence of a removal order first, then check the duration condition.
[P1] Do not conflate 'placed within last N hours' with 'in place for more than N hours'—these are opposite temporal filters.

[P1] Before concluding no action needed, verify time calculation with explicit day-to-hour conversion.

action_guidelines: [P1] When ordering a vaccine, use CVX (vaccine administered) or RxNorm coding system, not CPT.
[P1] If no prior vaccination records exist, do not order a booster without explicit protocol stating unvaccinated patients need one.
[P1] Before submitting a MedicationRequest, validate that the medication code belongs to an accepted standard system for the resource type.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent failed to find a CT Abdomen procedure because it searched only by CPT codes and then by text, missing the procedure coded with a different system. It did not first retrieve all procedures for the patient and filter by description.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent prematurely narrowed search using specific CPT codes and text search, missing the procedure because it was coded with a different code system. Should have retrieved all Procedure resources for the patient and then filtered by clinical description.
action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for a clinical procedure by type, first retrieve all Procedure resources for the patient, then filter by display name or text description rather than relying on a single code system.
[P1] If an initial code-based search returns no results, broaden the search to include all procedures for the patient before concluding absence.
[P1] Use the patient identifier directly in the search without assuming the absence of procedures from limited code searches.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent correctly identified two active heparin orders for DVT prophylaxis but failed to discontinue the duplicate because it did not perform a full read/update via PUT request, resulting in an invalid action.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent attempted to discontinue an order using a POST with status 'stopped' instead of performing a proper PUT update with the full resource, which is the required FHIR pattern for status changes.
action_guidelines: [P1] When updating a FHIR resource status (e.g., discontinuing an order), always use a PUT request with the complete updated resource, not a partial POST.
[P1] Before modifying an order, retrieve the latest full resource representation to ensure all required fields are included in the update.
[P1] When discontinuing duplicate orders, confirm the resource type's standard update pattern (PUT with full resource) rather than assuming a partial update is allowed.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent searched for CT Abdomen procedures using CPT codes, found two procedures, but incorrectly selected a 'CT Abdomen Pelvis Reference Only' as the most recent, missing that the task required a true CT Abdomen with contrast. The agent failed to verify that the procedure matched the required modality and contrast specification before concluding no new order was needed.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent selected a procedure that was not a true CT Abdomen (it was a reference-only study) and did not match the required contrast specification, leading to incorrect conclusion that threshold was not exceeded.
action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for a specific procedure type (e.g., CT Abdomen with IV contrast), ensure the selected procedure code corresponds to the exact modality and contrast specification, not just a related or reference study.
[P1] If multiple candidate procedures are found, prioritize those matching the exact requested codes or descriptions, and confirm the procedure type before assessing date thresholds.
[P1] Do not accept a procedure as the most recent unless its code or description unambiguously matches the requested study; use standard coding systems or explicit text matching.

[P1] If no evidence supports the condition, do not proceed with the dependent action; instead, report the gap or request clarification.

  "episodic_summary": "Agent verified DVT prophylaxis orders by checking for active LMWH orders, found exactly one, and concluded no action needed. However, the action was invalid because the agent did not first retrieve the patient's clinical condition or catheter insertion duration before proceeding.",
  "failure_analysis": "Agent skipped the mandatory step of retrieving and confirming the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) before initiating order verification, violating the current semantic rule P1.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "Before verifying order counts, always retrieve the patient's specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.",
    "When checking for DVT prophylaxis orders, search using therapeutic class (e.g., 'anticoagulants') and individual drug names to capture all relevant orders.",
    "After retrieving condition details, then check for active orders and apply the correct action (create, keep, or discontinue)."
  ],
  "tags": ["DVT prophylaxis", "order verification", "clinical condition prerequisite"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}

added: [P1] When verifying orders for DVT prophylaxis or similar preventive therapies, search by therapeutic class (e.g., 'anticoagulants') and individual drug names to capture all relevant orders.
removed: [P1] After retrieving condition details, then check for active orders and apply the correct action (create, keep, or discontinue).
kept: [P1] When searching for a procedure by name, verify the returned procedure code matches the exact requested exam type, including modality and contrast specification, not a related or reference-only variant.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC, CVX) not just text.
[P1] Compare all retrieved values against protocol-defined thresholds before deciding on action.
[P1] Treat absence only after exhaustive search; do not assume from a single resource type or code system.

[P1] When verifying active medications, search across all relevant medication types, resource types (MedicationRequest, MedicationAdministration, MedicationDispense), and code systems (e.g., RxNorm, generic, brand, drug class) and confirm search completeness before concluding absence.
[P1] When verifying active opioid orders, always search by therapeutic class (e.g., 'opioid analgesics') in addition to individual drug names to capture all formulations and coding variants.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.
[P1] When searching for procedures, use standard coding systems (e.g., SNOMED CT, LOINC) not local codes unless confirmed valid.
[P1] Do not assume a limited set of codes covers all possible representations of a procedure; use broad text or concept searches as fallback.
[P1] When creating a FHIR resource, validate against the server's required fields by reviewing prior successful POST examples or error responses.
[P1] If a requested lab has no results in records, explicitly note its absence and do not attempt protocol action that depends on those values.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.

[P1] Check for existing orders (e.g., removal orders) before creating a conflicting or duplicate request, searching across all relevant resource types (ServiceRequest, Procedure, Observation) and statuses (active, completed, entered-in-error) using appropriate removal codes or generic terms.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch all available results for each requested test, then select the most recent by date.

[P1] Before assessing whether a time threshold is met, retrieve the exact start timestamp from the relevant resource (e.g., Procedure, ServiceRequest) before comparing with current time; do not assume from absence or incomplete search.
[P1] Validate API endpoint and parameters before making a call; avoid invalid actions that abort the episode.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, explicitly list all matching dates from search results and pick the maximum.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields, invalid values, resource type mismatches, or coding system issues.
[P1] When verifying medication pairings, retrieve all active orders for the primary drug class first, then cross-check each against the secondary drug class.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.

[P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps for the maximum required range first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately; if no data exists for a window, return an explicit indicator (e.g., 'No data') rather than a fabricated value.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent attempted to create a catheter removal ServiceRequest without first verifying no existing removal order or procedure existed. The request used an invalid procedure code (NUR1373) as a ServiceRequest code and lacked required fields like requester.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it skipped the critical verification step: checking for existing removal orders before creating a new one. It also used a non-standard code system and omitted required fields.
action_guidelines: [P1] Before creating a removal request, search for existing ServiceRequest or Procedure resources with removal-related codes or statuses.
[P1] Use only standard coding systems (e.g., CPT, SNOMED, RxNorm) for procedure or service codes, not internal or non-standard identifiers.
[P1] When creating a FHIR resource, include all required fields (e.g., requester, subject reference, proper code system) as shown in prior successful POST examples.
[P1] Verify the patient identifier and the exact procedure code match the intended action before submitting.
[P1] If a verification step is specified (e.g., check for removal order), complete it fully before proceeding to action.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent was asked to check if a patient had a urinary catheter >48h without a removal order, and if so, create a removal request. Agent immediately created the request without first verifying the catheter duration or existing orders.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it skipped the prerequisite check for catheter duration and presence of a removal order, jumping directly to the action without confirming the condition.
action_guidelines: [P1] Before performing a conditional action, first verify all prerequisite conditions using available data (e.g., duration, existing orders).
[P1] If a condition requires checking device placement duration, retrieve relevant device or procedure records to confirm the start time.
[P1] Do not create a removal order until confirming no active or completed removal order already exists for the same device.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent attempted to compute average heart rate over 6h and 12h windows for patient S2450227. First query returned BP and SpO2, not heart rate. Agent then corrected by querying with HEARTRATE code, but action was invalid due to missing date format or endpoint issue.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent correctly identified need to filter by heart rate code, but the corrected query failed (invalid action), likely due to malformed date parameter or endpoint path.
action_guidelines: [P1] When a query returns observations of wrong type, explicitly filter by the exact observation code (e.g., HEARTRATE) in the next request.
[P1] Before executing a query, validate that the date parameter uses the correct ISO format (e.g., YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ) and the endpoint URL is properly constructed.
[P1] When computing averages over time windows, retrieve all relevant observations for the maximum required range in one call, then partition and compute per window.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent attempted to calculate average heart rate for patient S6500497 but only retrieved blood pressure data. It correctly identified the missing heart rate data but failed to adjust retrieval strategy or query for heart rate specifically.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent did not verify that the initial vitals query included heart rate observations; it accepted BP-only results without retrying with a corrected query parameter for heart rate.
action_guidelines: [P1] When computing averages over a time range, first confirm the retrieved observations contain the required vital sign type (e.g., heart rate), not just any vitals.
[P1] If initial query returns data for a different measurement type, adjust query parameters (e.g., observation code, category) to target the specific vital sign needed.
[P1] Before concluding data is absent, attempt a targeted query for the specific observation type requested.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent retrieved a 'CT Abdomen Pelvis Reference Only' code as the most recent procedure, but the instruction required a 'CT Abdomen with IV contrast'. The agent incorrectly deemed the time threshold unmet, but the core failure was using a mismatched procedure code.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent selected a reference-only variant code instead of the exact requested CT Abdomen with contrast, leading to an incorrect procedure date and potentially invalid conclusion.
action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for a procedure, verify the returned code matches the exact exam type, including modality, contrast, and body part; reject reference-only or composite variants.
[P1] If no exact match exists in search results, report that no matching procedure was found rather than using a related code.
[P1] Before assessing time thresholds, confirm the procedure code matches the requested exam specification.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent searched for COVID-19 vaccination records across Immunization, Procedure, and MedicationRequest. Found none, assumed last dose >12 months ago, and ordered a booster. The action was invalid, likely due to incomplete search (e.g., missing Observation resource).
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it did not search all possible FHIR resources that can document vaccinations (e.g., Observation), leading to a false negative. It then incorrectly concluded no prior vaccine existed and proceeded to order.
action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for vaccination records, query all relevant FHIR resources: Immunization, Procedure, MedicationRequest, and Observation.
[P1] If no records are found across all expected resource types, do not automatically assume the condition is met; consider that data may exist elsewhere or the search parameters may be incomplete.
[P1] Before ordering a new procedure or medication based on absence of prior data, verify that the search covered all plausible FHIR resource types for that clinical event.

reflection_raw:
```json
{
  "episodic_summary": "Agent searched for COVID-19 vaccination records across Immunization, Procedure, and MedicationRequest. Found none, assumed last dose >12 months ago, and ordered a booster. The action was invalid, likely due to incomplete search (e.g., missing Observation resource).",
  "failure_analysis": "Agent failed because it did not search all possible FHIR resources that can document vaccinations (e.g., Observation), leading to a false negative. It then incorrectly concluded no prior vaccine existed and proceeded to order.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "When searching for vaccination records, query all relevant FHIR resources: Immunization, Procedure, MedicationRequest, and Observation.",
    "If no records are found across all expected resource types, do not automatically assume the condition is met; consider that data may exist elsewhere or the search parameters may be incomplete.",
    "Before ordering a new procedure or medication based on absence of prior data, verify that the search covered all plausible FHIR resource types for that clinical event."
  ],
  "tags": ["vaccination", "search strategy", "resource coverage", "false negative"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}
```

added: [P1] When searching for clinical events (e.g., vaccinations, procedures), query all plausible FHIR resource types (e.g., Immunization, Procedure, MedicationRequest, Observation) before concluding absence; a negative from a single resource type is insufficient.
kept: [P1] Compare all retrieved values against protocol-defined thresholds before deciding on action.
[P1] Treat absence only after exhaustive search; do not assume from a single resource type or code system.

[P1] When verifying active medications, search across all relevant medication types, resource types (MedicationRequest, MedicationAdministration, MedicationDispense), and code systems (e.g., RxNorm, generic, brand, drug class) and confirm search completeness before concluding absence.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.
[P1] When searching for procedures, use standard coding systems (e.g., SNOMED CT, LOINC) not local codes unless confirmed valid.
[P1] When verifying active opioid orders, always search by therapeutic class (e.g., 'opioid analgesics') in addition to individual drug names to capture all formulations and coding variants.

[P1] When searching for a procedure by name, verify the returned procedure code matches the exact requested exam type, including modality and contrast specification, not a related or reference-only variant.
[P1] Do not assume a limited set of codes covers all possible representations of a procedure; use broad text or concept searches as fallback.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC, CVX) not just text.
[P1] If a requested lab has no results in records, explicitly note its absence and do not attempt protocol action that depends on those values.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.
[P1] When creating a FHIR resource, validate against the server's required fields by reviewing prior successful POST examples or error responses.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.

[P1] Check for existing orders (e.g., removal orders) before creating a conflicting or duplicate request, searching across all relevant resource types (ServiceRequest, Procedure, Observation) and statuses (active, completed, entered-in-error) using appropriate removal codes or generic terms.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch all available results for each requested test, then select the most recent by date.

[P1] Before assessing whether a time threshold is met, retrieve the exact start timestamp from the relevant resource (e.g., Procedure, ServiceRequest) before comparing with current time; do not assume from absence or incomplete search.
[P1] Validate API endpoint and parameters before making a call; avoid invalid actions that abort the episode.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, explicitly list all matching dates from search results and pick the maximum.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields, invalid values, resource type mismatches, or coding system issues.
[P1] When verifying medication pairings, retrieve all active orders for the primary drug class first, then cross-check each against the secondary drug class.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.

[P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps for the maximum required range first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately; if no data exists for a window, return an explicit indicator (e.g., 'No data') rather than a fabricated value.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.
[P1] Only order a new procedure after confirming the most recent date exceeds the threshold; do not assume absence from limited code search.

[P1] If no results are found for a requested lab, explicitly state the absence and do not proceed with protocol-dependent actions.
[P1] Before finishing, confirm that all requested information has been retrieved and evaluated.

[P1] Do not treat a 'verify' step as complete until the corresponding action (create, discontinue, or confirm) is explicitly executed.
[P1] When the instruction specifies discrete outcomes, simulate each outcome's check and only terminate after the appropriate action is taken.

[P1] Do not assume absence of records means the event never occurred; verify with multiple search strategies.
[P1] When ordering a vaccine, ensure the patient identity is confirmed and the vaccine code uses a standard coding system.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent attempted to find CT Abdomen procedure date but searched using incorrect CPT codes, resulting in no matches. The agent correctly identified the need to search by procedure code but used wrong codes, leading to failure.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent used incorrect CPT codes for CT Abdomen (IMGCT0491, IMGIL0001) instead of the correct codes, causing the search to return no results. The agent should have verified the correct codes before querying.
action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for a procedure by name, first look up the correct standard code (e.g., CPT, SNOMED) for that specific exam, including modality and contrast, before querying.
[P1] If a code-based search returns no results, verify the codes are correct by checking the terminology or FHIR CodeSystem before concluding the procedure is absent.
[P1] When instructed to find a procedure date, use both code and text-based searches to ensure comprehensive coverage.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent was asked to check for a urinary catheter in place >48 hours without removal order, and create a removal request if so. It searched for procedure code NUR1373, found only an old insertion, concluded no active catheter, and did not create the order. The action was invalid because the search logic was incorrect.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly searched for the catheter insertion procedure using a code (NUR1373) that represents the catheter itself, not the insertion procedure. It also applied a date filter that excluded the relevant time window, failing to identify the active catheter. The condition required checking for a catheter placed >48 hours ago, not searching for recent insertions.
action_guidelines: [P1] When checking for an indwelling device, search for the device itself (e.g., using Device resource) rather than the insertion procedure, and verify its current status and placement date.
[P1] When a condition depends on a time threshold (e.g., '>48 hours'), calculate the threshold datetime from the current time, then search for devices placed before that threshold, not after.
[P1] If a procedure code search is appropriate, verify the code represents the correct event (insertion, maintenance, removal) not just the device or item.
[P1] When a search returns no recent results, re-examine whether the search criteria (code, date range, resource type) are correctly aligned with the clinical question before concluding absence.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent searched for a urinary catheter procedure using code NUR1373, got no results, and concluded no catheter existed. The search was too narrow; catheters may be recorded with different codes or as devices, not procedures. The agent failed to explore alternative representations.
reflection.failure_analysis: The agent assumed a single code (NUR1373) was the only way to represent a urinary catheter. It did not search for related device records, condition codes, or broader procedure terms. The absence of one code does not prove absence of the clinical reality.
action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for a medical device or implant, search across multiple resource types (Device, Procedure, Condition, Observation) using multiple synonymous codes or display names.
[P1] If a specific code returns no results, broaden the search to include related codes, free-text terms, or alternative code systems before concluding the device is absent.
[P1] For time-sensitive conditions (e.g., catheter duration), always check the relevant date fields (performed, recorded, or inserted) on any matching records, not just existence.

{
  "episodic_summary": "Agent searched for CT Abdomen procedures using specific CPT codes but found no results and failed to proceed. The agent did not verify the codes or check alternative coding systems before concluding.",
  "failure_analysis": "Agent assumed the codes were correct without verification, leading to empty search results. Missed step to validate procedure codes via terminology lookup before querying.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "Before searching with procedure codes, verify the codes exist in the relevant terminology system (e.g., FHIR CodeSystem) to avoid empty results.",
    "If a code-based search returns no results, do not stop; instead, check for alternative codes or synonyms for the same procedure.",
    "When searching for a procedure, start with broader terms (e.g., 'CT Abdomen') then refine with codes only after confirming the correct mapping."
  ],
  "tags": ["code verification", "procedure search", "empty result handling"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}

added: [P1] Before searching with procedure codes, verify the codes exist in the relevant terminology system (e.g., FHIR CodeSystem) to avoid empty results; if no results, check for alternative codes or synonyms.
removed: [P1] If a code-based search returns no results, verify the codes are correct by checking the terminology or FHIR CodeSystem before concluding the procedure is absent.
[P1] If a code-based search returns no results, do not stop; instead, check for alternative codes or synonyms for the same procedure.
[P1] When searching for a procedure, start with broader terms (e.g., 'CT Abdomen') then refine with codes only after confirming the correct mapping.
kept: [P1] When checking for existing orders, search across all relevant resource types (MedicationRequest, ServiceRequest, Procedure, Observation) and all possible statuses (active, draft, completed, entered-in-error) to avoid missing or duplicating orders.
[P1] Compare all retrieved values against protocol-defined thresholds before deciding on action.
[P1] Treat absence only after exhaustive search; do not assume from a single resource type or code system.

[P1] When verifying active orders, search by therapeutic class (e.g., 'opioid analgesics', 'anticoagulants', 'DVT prophylaxis') in addition to individual drug names to capture all formulations and coding variants.

[P1] When verifying active medications, search across all relevant medication types, resource types (MedicationRequest, MedicationAdministration, MedicationDispense), and code systems (e.g., RxNorm, generic, brand, drug class) and confirm search completeness before concluding absence.
[P1] Apply protocol action only after confirming all relevant lab values are retrieved and evaluated.
[P1] When searching for procedures, use standard coding systems (e.g., SNOMED CT, LOINC) not local codes unless confirmed valid.
[P1] When searching for a procedure by name, first look up the correct standard code (e.g., CPT, SNOMED) for that specific exam, including modality and contrast, before querying.
[P1] Do not assume a limited set of codes covers all possible representations of a procedure; use broad text or concept searches as fallback.
[P1] Before initiating any action, first retrieve and confirm the specific clinical condition or duration (e.g., catheter insertion time) from records.
[P1] Do not conflate 'no records found' with 'records exist but are too old'; each case has different implications for ordering.
[P1] When creating a FHIR resource, validate against the server's required fields by reviewing prior successful POST examples or error responses.
[P1] If a requested lab has no results in records, explicitly note its absence and do not attempt protocol action that depends on those values.
[P1] When instructed to retrieve lab values, always fetch all available results for each requested test, then select the most recent by date.

[P1] Before assessing whether a time threshold is met, retrieve the exact start timestamp from the relevant resource (e.g., Procedure, ServiceRequest) before comparing with current time; do not assume from absence or incomplete search.
[P1] Validate API endpoint and parameters before making a call; avoid invalid actions that abort the episode.
[P1] When asked for the most recent date, explicitly list all matching dates from search results and pick the maximum.
[P1] Before creating a new order, confirm the patient identifier and ensure the medication code uses a standard system (e.g., RxNorm, NDC, CVX) not just text.
[P1] If a POST action fails, examine the server error response for missing required fields, invalid values, resource type mismatches, or coding system issues.
[P1] When verifying medication pairings, retrieve all active orders for the primary drug class first, then cross-check each against the secondary drug class.
[P1] If initial query returns unrelated data, check query parameters (code, patient, date range) and retry with corrected parameters.
[P1] Apply date thresholds (e.g., 12 months) only after determining the most recent procedure date.

[P1] When computing averages over multiple time windows, retrieve all relevant observations with timestamps for the maximum required range first, then filter per window and compute each mean separately; if no data exists for a window, return an explicit indicator (e.g., 'No data') rather than a fabricated value.
[P1] When a verification step is explicitly required, do not proceed to the action until all conditions are confirmed met.

added_rules: [P1] Before searching with procedure codes, verify the codes exist in the relevant terminology system (e.g., FHIR CodeSystem) to avoid empty results; if no results, check for alternative codes or synonyms.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent requested heart rate observations but received BP and FiO2 data, then corrected query to include code parameter. Agent failed to compute averages due to missing heart rate data or query issue.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent succeeded in identifying missing heart rate data and correcting the query, but failed to retrieve valid heart rate observations, likely due to incorrect code parameter (HEARTRATE instead of standard code like 8867-4 or 8480-6).
action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for vital signs, use standard LOINC codes (e.g., heart rate = 8867-4 or 8480-6) instead of text labels.
[P1] If initial query returns no data, verify the code system and try alternative standard codes or synonyms.
[P1] After retrieving observations, verify temporal coverage before computing averages across multiple windows.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent attempted to find most recent CT Abdomen date but used invalid CPT codes (IMGCT0491, IMGIL0001). Then switched to text-based search, which is unreliable. Failed to verify codes in a terminology system before querying.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent used non-standard or incorrect CPT codes without first verifying them in a FHIR CodeSystem, leading to empty results and an invalid action.
action_guidelines: [P1] Before using any procedure code, verify its existence in the relevant FHIR CodeSystem (e.g., CPT, SNOMED).
[P1] If a code search returns zero results, do not fall back to text search alone; instead, search for alternative standard codes or synonyms.
[P1] When searching for imaging procedures, use the standardized code system (e.g., CPT) with correct codes for modality and contrast, not arbitrary internal identifiers.

reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it treated missing vaccination records as definitive absence of need, rather than investigating further (e.g., checking alternative sources, verifying patient identity, or considering that missing data may require a new order per protocol).

}

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent checked for existing flu vaccine by searching MedicationRequest with status=completed, found one from 2021-10-10, determined >365 days, then ordered a new vaccine using CPT code 90686 via POST. POST failed due to invalid action.
reflection.failure_analysis: The agent used a CPT code in a MedicationRequest resource, which requires RxNorm or CVX codes, not CPT. The POST failed due to coding system mismatch.
action_guidelines: [P1] When ordering a vaccine, use CVX (vaccine administered) or RxNorm codes, not CPT or SNOMED procedure codes.
[P1] After determining a new order is needed, verify the medication code system matches the resource type requirements before POSTing.
[P1] Check the server error response for code system validation errors when a POST fails.

```json
  ],

  "should_update_semantic": true
}
```

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent checked for active opioid medications by individual drug names only, found none, and concluded no naloxone needed. Missed opioid orders present under therapeutic class codes (e.g., 'opioid analgesics'), leading to a false negative.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent searched by specific drug names but not by therapeutic class (e.g., 'opioid analgesics'), missing active orders coded under class-level terminology. Guideline P5 (search by therapeutic class) was not applied.
action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for medications of a class, always query by the therapeutic class code (e.g., 'opioid analgesics', 'anticoagulants') in addition to individual drug names to capture all formulations and coding variants.
[P1] Before concluding a drug class is absent, verify you have searched using both class-level and drug-level terminology systems (e.g., RxNorm, generic, brand).
[P1] If no results from a drug-name search, re-query using the parent therapeutic class to catch coded variants missed by name-only lookups.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent found no prior COVID-19 vaccination records for patient, interpreted absence as meeting 'more than 12 months ago' condition, and ordered a booster. The task succeeded but used a fragile assumption.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent succeeded because the instruction implicitly allowed ordering a booster when no prior dose existed, but this conflates 'no record' with 'dose >12 months old'. A more robust approach would explicitly confirm vaccination history is empty before ordering.
action_guidelines: [P1] When checking vaccination status, first attempt to retrieve all immunization records for the patient; if no records exist, treat as 'no prior dose' and order the booster only if the instruction explicitly covers that case.
[P1] Do not assume absence of records is equivalent to 'last dose more than 12 months ago' unless the task definition confirms that interpretation.
[P1] For date-based conditional logic, always compare against actual found dates; avoid inferring missing data as meeting the condition.

  "episodic_summary": "Agent found no prior COVID-19 vaccination records for patient, interpreted absence as meeting 'more than 12 months ago' condition, and ordered a booster. The task succeeded but used a fragile assumption.",
  "failure_analysis": "Agent succeeded because the instruction implicitly allowed ordering a booster when no prior dose existed, but this conflates 'no record' with 'dose >12 months old'. A more robust approach would explicitly confirm vaccination history is empty before ordering.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "When checking vaccination status, first attempt to retrieve all immunization records for the patient; if no records exist, treat as 'no prior dose' and order the booster only if the instruction explicitly covers that case.",
    "Do not assume absence of records is equivalent to 'last dose more than 12 months ago' unless the task definition confirms that interpretation.",

[P1] Do not create an order until you have confirmed zero active orders through at least two different search approaches.
